course.title: Advanced Algorithm Design in Python
course.description: Developers who already implement foundational structures and now need proof-driven graph, optimization, randomized, approximation, and feasibility techniques. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate proof techniques, amortized analysis, balanced trees, and disjoint sets through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate strong components, shortest paths, spanning trees, greedy design, and dynamic programming through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate backtracking, string algorithms, network flow, matching, randomization, and approximation through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate complexity boundaries, feasibility communication, resource budgets, and advanced review defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
misconceptions: Checking several sizes completes an induction proof.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.

competencies.statement: State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
misconceptions: A loop invariant only needs to be true after the loop.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.

competencies.statement: Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.
misconceptions: Exchange arguments apply to every greedy choice automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.

competencies.statement: Solve recurrences with substitution, recursion trees, or master-method conditions.
misconceptions: Master theorem handles every recurrence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Solve recurrences with substitution, recursion trees, or master-method conditions.

competencies.statement: Argue comparison or information lower bounds without confusing them with one implementation.
misconceptions: A slow implementation proves a problem lower bound.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Argue comparison or information lower bounds without confusing them with one implementation.

competencies.statement: Bound total operation cost over a sequence and derive amortized cost.
misconceptions: Amortized cost is the same as average random-case cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound total operation cost over a sequence and derive amortized cost.

competencies.statement: Assign credits that cover future expensive operations without negative balances.
misconceptions: Credits represent actual runtime memory.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign credits that cover future expensive operations without negative balances.

competencies.statement: Define a nonnegative potential and calculate amortized cost changes.
misconceptions: Any quantity can serve as a valid potential function.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a nonnegative potential and calculate amortized cost changes.

competencies.statement: Compare growth and shrink thresholds for time, slack, and thrashing behavior.
misconceptions: Halving capacity whenever half full is always safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare growth and shrink thresholds for time, slack, and thrashing behavior.

competencies.statement: Construct operation sequences that expose weak amortized claims and latency spikes.
misconceptions: A benign benchmark validates every operation sequence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct operation sequences that expose weak amortized claims and latency spikes.

competencies.statement: Trace rotations while preserving binary-search order and reconnecting every affected link.
misconceptions: A rotation changes the inorder key sequence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace rotations while preserving binary-search order and reconnecting every affected link.

competencies.statement: Maintain AVL heights and select single or double rotations from imbalance shape.
misconceptions: Any rotation at an imbalanced node restores AVL balance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain AVL heights and select single or double rotations from imbalance shape.

competencies.statement: Use red-black color properties to bound height and guide update repair.
misconceptions: A red-black tree is perfectly balanced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use red-black color properties to bound height and guide update repair.

competencies.statement: Maintain subtree aggregates for rank, select, interval, or range queries.
misconceptions: Adding cached fields cannot affect update complexity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain subtree aggregates for rank, select, interval, or range queries.

competencies.statement: Compare balanced trees with sorted arrays, heaps, and hash tables by workload.
misconceptions: Balanced trees dominate hash tables for every lookup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare balanced trees with sorted arrays, heaps, and hash tables by workload.

competencies.statement: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.
misconceptions: Every element directly points to the representative.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.

competencies.statement: Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.
misconceptions: Rank always equals exact tree height after compression.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.

competencies.statement: Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.
misconceptions: Path compression makes every operation strict constant time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.

competencies.statement: Interpret the inverse-Ackermann amortized bound without presenting it as a worst-case constant.
misconceptions: A tiny growth function means analysis is unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret the inverse-Ackermann amortized bound without presenting it as a worst-case constant.

competencies.statement: Use disjoint sets for batch connectivity while recognizing unsupported deletion and path needs.
misconceptions: Union-find can return actual graph paths.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use disjoint sets for batch connectivity while recognizing unsupported deletion and path needs.

competencies.statement: Use discovery and finish structure to reason about directed reachability.
misconceptions: DFS timestamps depend only on graph structure, not traversal order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use discovery and finish structure to reason about directed reachability.

competencies.statement: Compute strongly connected components and validate mutual reachability within each result.
misconceptions: Connected components and strong components are identical.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute strongly connected components and validate mutual reachability within each result.

competencies.statement: Collapse strong components into an acyclic condensation graph.
misconceptions: Collapsing cycles loses all useful dependency information.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Collapse strong components into an acyclic condensation graph.

competencies.statement: Return a concrete directed cycle witness for invalid dependency plans.
misconceptions: A boolean cycle flag is sufficient operational evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Return a concrete directed cycle witness for invalid dependency plans.

competencies.statement: Use DAG order with readiness and parallel-resource constraints without implying arbitrary tasks can overlap.
misconceptions: Topological order is a complete scheduler.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use DAG order with readiness and parallel-resource constraints without implying arbitrary tasks can overlap.

competencies.statement: State and apply edge relaxation while preserving predecessor evidence.
misconceptions: Relaxing an edge permanently finalizes its endpoint.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State and apply edge relaxation while preserving predecessor evidence.

competencies.statement: Run Dijkstra only under compatible nonnegative weights and stale-entry handling.
misconceptions: Dijkstra works with negative edges if no negative cycle exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Dijkstra only under compatible nonnegative weights and stale-entry handling.

competencies.statement: Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.
misconceptions: Bellman-Ford reports every negative cycle in the graph.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.

competencies.statement: Exploit topological order for weighted DAG shortest paths including negative edges.
misconceptions: Negative weights always require Bellman-Ford.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Exploit topological order for weighted DAG shortest paths including negative edges.

competencies.statement: Validate reported cost, edge sequence, reachability, and algorithm preconditions.
misconceptions: Matching a distance value proves the reconstructed path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate reported cost, edge sequence, reachability, and algorithm preconditions.

competencies.statement: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.
misconceptions: The lightest remaining edge is always safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.

competencies.statement: Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.
misconceptions: Kruskal requires a connected input to produce useful output.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.

competencies.statement: Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.
misconceptions: Prim and Dijkstra maintain the same path distances.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.

competencies.statement: Detect ties and avoid claiming uniqueness without sufficient weight conditions.
misconceptions: Every weighted graph has one unique MST.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect ties and avoid claiming uniqueness without sufficient weight conditions.

competencies.statement: Distinguish minimum construction cost from shortest routes, resilience, and capacity.
misconceptions: An MST is the best operational network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish minimum construction cost from shortest routes, resilience, and capacity.

competencies.statement: Define a locally optimal choice and the remaining subproblem precisely.
misconceptions: Choosing the largest immediate gain is universally greedy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a locally optimal choice and the remaining subproblem precisely.

competencies.statement: Distinguish optimal substructure from the stronger greedy-choice property.
misconceptions: Optimal substructure guarantees a greedy solution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish optimal substructure from the stronger greedy-choice property.

competencies.statement: Transform an optimal solution to include the greedy choice without worsening it.
misconceptions: An exchange proof may assume the transformed solution remains feasible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transform an optimal solution to include the greedy choice without worsening it.

competencies.statement: Construct smallest counterexamples for plausible but invalid heuristics.
misconceptions: A heuristic that works on sample data is a greedy algorithm.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct smallest counterexamples for plausible but invalid heuristics.

competencies.statement: Apply proven greedy structures to interval scheduling and prefix-code construction.
misconceptions: All interval problems use earliest-start choice.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply proven greedy structures to interval scheduling and prefix-code construction.

competencies.statement: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
misconceptions: More state dimensions always make a DP more correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.

competencies.statement: Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
misconceptions: A recurrence is correct because it resembles known DP code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.

competencies.statement: Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
misconceptions: Top-down and bottom-up always evaluate identical states.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.

competencies.statement: Store or recompute choices to recover an optimal solution, not only its value.
misconceptions: The optimal score uniquely identifies chosen items.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store or recompute choices to recover an optimal solution, not only its value.

competencies.statement: Prove state meaning and transition completeness by induction.
misconceptions: Testing many inputs replaces a DP correctness argument.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prove state meaning and transition completeness by induction.

competencies.statement: Model longest-common-subsequence and edit-distance choices with boundary rows and reconstruction.
misconceptions: Greedy character matching always yields a longest subsequence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model longest-common-subsequence and edit-distance choices with boundary rows and reconstruction.

competencies.statement: Distinguish zero-one, unbounded, subset-sum, and value-indexed knapsack states.
misconceptions: One knapsack loop order works for every variant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish zero-one, unbounded, subset-sum, and value-indexed knapsack states.

competencies.statement: Compress DP rows only when overwritten states are no longer needed.
misconceptions: Any two-dimensional DP can use one row safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compress DP rows only when overwritten states are no longer needed.

competencies.statement: Derive iteration order from transition dependencies rather than coding habit.
misconceptions: Loop order affects speed but not semantics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive iteration order from transition dependencies rather than coding habit.

competencies.statement: Estimate pseudo-polynomial state growth and reject impractical exact models.
misconceptions: Polynomial in a numeric value is polynomial in input length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate pseudo-polynomial state growth and reject impractical exact models.

competencies.statement: Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.
misconceptions: Copying all state is required for correct backtracking.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.

competencies.statement: Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.
misconceptions: A branch that looks unlikely may be discarded safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.

competencies.statement: Use constrained-first and value-order heuristics without confusing speed with correctness.
misconceptions: A heuristic ordering changes the solution set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use constrained-first and value-order heuristics without confusing speed with correctness.

competencies.statement: Maintain incumbent solutions and optimistic bounds for optimization search.
misconceptions: Any estimated score is a valid pruning bound.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain incumbent solutions and optimistic bounds for optimization search.

competencies.statement: Measure expanded nodes, pruned reasons, depth, and incumbent improvements.
misconceptions: Elapsed time alone explains search behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure expanded nodes, pruned reasons, depth, and incumbent improvements.

competencies.statement: Compute prefix-function fallback lengths while preserving matched-prefix meaning.
misconceptions: Fallback always resets matching to zero.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute prefix-function fallback lengths while preserving matched-prefix meaning.

competencies.statement: Use prefix evidence to avoid rechecking characters in Knuth-Morris-Pratt search.
misconceptions: KMP skips text characters that might begin a match.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use prefix evidence to avoid rechecking characters in Knuth-Morris-Pratt search.

competencies.statement: Use rolling hashes with explicit modulus, collision verification, and numeric limits.
misconceptions: Equal hashes prove equal substrings.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use rolling hashes with explicit modulus, collision verification, and numeric limits.

competencies.statement: Extend trie state with failure transitions for multi-pattern matching.
misconceptions: Running one trie lookup from each text position is linear.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Extend trie state with failure transitions for multi-pattern matching.

competencies.statement: Construct repeated-prefix, Unicode, empty-pattern, and collision-heavy cases.
misconceptions: Natural-language samples expose worst-case string behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct repeated-prefix, Unicode, empty-pattern, and collision-heavy cases.

competencies.statement: Translate capacities, conservation, source, sink, and residual choices from a stakeholder problem.
misconceptions: Every graph optimization problem is naturally a flow network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate capacities, conservation, source, sink, and residual choices from a stakeholder problem.

competencies.statement: Trace forward and reverse residual capacity after augmentation.
misconceptions: A reverse residual edge means physical flow moves backward.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace forward and reverse residual capacity after augmentation.

competencies.statement: Find augmenting paths and update flow without violating capacity or conservation.
misconceptions: Any source-to-sink path increases flow by one.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find augmenting paths and update flow without violating capacity or conservation.

competencies.statement: Use a final residual reachability cut as optimality evidence.
misconceptions: Maximum flow and minimum cut are the same edge set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a final residual reachability cut as optimality evidence.

competencies.statement: Reduce bipartite matching to flow and reconstruct matched stakeholder pairs.
misconceptions: A maximum matching assigns every participant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reduce bipartite matching to flow and reconstruct matched stakeholder pairs.

competencies.statement: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.
misconceptions: Randomized algorithms are merely nondeterministic heuristics.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.

competencies.statement: Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.
misconceptions: Random pivots guarantee balanced partitions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.

competencies.statement: Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.
misconceptions: Repeating correlated trials multiplies confidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.

competencies.statement: State objective direction and worst-case approximation ratio relative to optimum.
misconceptions: A solution within ten units has a ten-percent guarantee.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State objective direction and worst-case approximation ratio relative to optimum.

competencies.statement: Report assumptions, randomness source, error chance, quality bound, and observed evidence separately.
misconceptions: Observed good quality proves the theoretical guarantee.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Report assumptions, randomness source, error chance, quality bound, and observed evidence separately.

competencies.statement: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.
misconceptions: Decision and optimization versions always have identical implementations.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.

competencies.statement: Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.
misconceptions: NP means not polynomial.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.

competencies.statement: Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.
misconceptions: Reducing an easy problem to a hard problem proves the easy one hard.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.

competencies.statement: Choose exact bounded, parameterized, approximation, or heuristic responses from constraints.
misconceptions: NP-hard means no useful solution can exist.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose exact bounded, parameterized, approximation, or heuristic responses from constraints.

competencies.statement: Explain computational limits with options and evidence rather than jargon or certainty.
misconceptions: Naming NP-hardness ends stakeholder discussion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain computational limits with options and evidence rather than jargon or certainty.

competencies.statement: Compare exact, approximate, randomized, and baseline candidates under one evidence model.
misconceptions: Only the asymptotically best candidate deserves implementation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare exact, approximate, randomized, and baseline candidates under one evidence model.

competencies.statement: Connect proof obligations to executable invariants, oracle comparisons, and counterexamples.
misconceptions: Formal reasoning and testing are interchangeable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect proof obligations to executable invariants, oracle comparisons, and counterexamples.

competencies.statement: Enforce time, memory, recursion, and output budgets under adversarial scale.
misconceptions: Average benchmark performance sets a safe resource ceiling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce time, memory, recursion, and output budgets under adversarial scale.

competencies.statement: Design timeout, partial-result, retry, and diagnostic behavior without corrupting claims.
misconceptions: An algorithm failure should always retry unchanged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design timeout, partial-result, retry, and diagnostic behavior without corrupting claims.

competencies.statement: Defend correctness, cost, alternatives, limits, and operational behavior to a technical review board.
misconceptions: A complex implementation demonstrates advanced mastery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend correctness, cost, alternatives, limits, and operational behavior to a technical review board.

credential.title: Advanced Algorithm Design in Python Certificate

modules.title: Proof Techniques, Invariants, and Recurrence Bounds
modules.description: Review a proposed optimization whose runtime and correctness claims lack evidence. Learners cumulatively produce a formal algorithm review packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.

modules.title: Amortized Analysis and Dynamic Structures
modules.description: Guarantee long-run latency for a structure with occasional expensive rebuilds. Learners cumulatively produce an amortized-cost ledger and implementation through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bound total operation cost over a sequence and derive amortized cost.
Assign credits that cover future expensive operations without negative balances.
Define a nonnegative potential and calculate amortized cost changes.

modules.title: Balanced Search Trees and Order Statistics
modules.description: Maintain an ordered index with predictable latency during sorted and mixed updates. Learners cumulatively produce a balanced-tree trace and order-statistic extension through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace rotations while preserving binary-search order and reconnecting every affected link.
Maintain AVL heights and select single or double rotations from imbalance shape.
Use red-black color properties to bound height and guide update repair.

modules.title: Disjoint Sets, Connectivity, and Offline Grouping
modules.description: Track changing network connectivity across merges without scanning every member. Learners cumulatively produce a union-find service with measured compression through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.
Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.
Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.

modules.title: Directed Graphs, Strong Components, and Condensation
modules.description: Analyze circular dependencies and group mutually reachable services. Learners cumulatively produce a strongly-connected dependency report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use discovery and finish structure to reason about directed reachability.
Compute strongly connected components and validate mutual reachability within each result.
Collapse strong components into an acyclic condensation graph.

modules.title: Weighted Shortest Paths and Relaxation
modules.description: Choose safe routes across positive, negative, and unreachable weighted networks. Learners cumulatively produce a multi-algorithm route engine with path proofs through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: State and apply edge relaxation while preserving predecessor evidence.
Run Dijkstra only under compatible nonnegative weights and stale-entry handling.
Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.

modules.title: Minimum Spanning Trees and Network Design
modules.description: Connect sites at minimum build cost while explaining redundancy limits. Learners cumulatively produce a minimum-network design and sensitivity brief through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use safe-edge cut arguments to justify minimum-spanning-tree choices.
Run Kruskal with deterministic edge ordering and disjoint-set cycle prevention.
Run Prim with frontier priorities and stale-entry handling.

modules.title: Greedy Algorithms and Exchange Arguments
modules.description: Allocate rooms, bandwidth, and deadlines under competing local choices. Learners cumulatively produce a greedy-design decision and proof notebook through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a locally optimal choice and the remaining subproblem precisely.
Distinguish optimal substructure from the stronger greedy-choice property.
Transform an optimal solution to include the greedy choice without worsening it.

modules.title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction
modules.description: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Learners cumulatively produce a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.

modules.title: Advanced Dynamic Programming and State Compression
modules.description: Scale sequence alignment and constrained-selection models beyond naive tables. Learners cumulatively produce an optimized DP with equivalence evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model longest-common-subsequence and edit-distance choices with boundary rows and reconstruction.
Distinguish zero-one, unbounded, subset-sum, and value-indexed knapsack states.
Compress DP rows only when overwritten states are no longer needed.

modules.title: Backtracking, Constraint Propagation, and Branch-and-Bound
modules.description: Search schedules and assignments while proving why pruned branches cannot help. Learners cumulatively produce a bounded combinatorial solver with pruning audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define partial assignments, legal extensions, completion, and undo invariants.
Prune only with a condition that cannot remove a valid or better solution.
Use constrained-first and value-order heuristics without confusing speed with correctness.

modules.title: Linear-Time String Search and Prefix Structure
modules.description: Scan large logs for many repeated patterns without repeated restart work. Learners cumulatively produce a verified prefix-based matching engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compute prefix-function fallback lengths while preserving matched-prefix meaning.
Use prefix evidence to avoid rechecking characters in Knuth-Morris-Pratt search.
Use rolling hashes with explicit modulus, collision verification, and numeric limits.

modules.title: Network Flow, Cuts, and Bipartite Matching
modules.description: Assign workers to constrained jobs and identify capacity bottlenecks. Learners cumulatively produce a flow-based assignment model and cut explanation through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate capacities, conservation, source, sink, and residual choices from a stakeholder problem.
Trace forward and reverse residual capacity after augmentation.
Find augmenting paths and update flow without violating capacity or conservation.

modules.title: Randomized Algorithms, Approximation, and Honest Guarantees
modules.description: Choose scalable methods when exact optimization is too expensive or adversarial. Learners cumulatively produce a probabilistic and approximation guarantee report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish Las Vegas and Monte Carlo guarantees, failure probability, and expected cost.
Analyze random pivot selection against input-order adversaries and seeded tests.
Reduce independent error probability through repeated trials and justified aggregation.

modules.title: Complexity Classes, Reductions, and Feasibility Boundaries
modules.description: Explain why a requested exact optimizer needs changed scope or a bounded alternative. Learners cumulatively produce a feasibility and reduction decision memo through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.
Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.
Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.

modules.title: Advanced Algorithm Engineering Capstone
modules.description: Build and defend a planning engine across graph, optimization, and uncertain-load requirements. Learners cumulatively produce an advanced algorithm system with proof and operations packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compare exact, approximate, randomized, and baseline candidates under one evidence model.
Connect proof obligations to executable invariants, oracle comparisons, and counterexamples.
Enforce time, memory, recursion, and output budgets under adversarial scale.
